front: Novel: the slaves Cora and Caesar escape a Georgia plantation and are pursued by the slave catcher Ridgeway along a literal subway beneath the earth
Novel: in South Carolina, the protagonist discovers forced sterilization by Dr. Stevens and syphilis experiments on Black men
Novel: the protagonist hides in an attic in North Carolina with Martin and Ethel, who are both killed; the state has enacted laws to execute all runaways
Novel: Elwood Curtis is falsely accused and sent to a Florida reform school inspired by the Dozier School; his friend Jack Turner later gives testimony
Novel: the work shed called the White House is nicknamed the 'Ice Cream Factory' due to the bruising students receive
Novel: Griff is killed for failing to throw a boxing match, pleading 'I thought it was the second round!'
Novel: the protagonist wins fake encyclopedias (only volume 'A' is real) in a dish-drying competition at a reform school
Novel: Lila Mae Watson is the first Black female elevator inspector in a conflict between 'Intuitionists' and 'Empiricists'
Novel: a 'sweeper' nicknamed Mark Spitz cleans up zombies ('skels') in Manhattan and suffers from Post-Apocalyptic Stress Disorder
Author: parodied critic James Wood's style in 'Wow, Fiction Works!' after a patronizing review; claimed 'the quick brown fox' is a 'perfect sentence'
Author: wrote 'Hard Times in the Uncanny Valley' for Grantland about the 2012 Olympics, traveling with a W.G. Sebald companion app
